title: Church Administrator Job at Boat Recruitment Agency
content:
Boat Recruitment Agency is a reputed company providing recruitment services and aims to ensure optimal client satisfaction. We also have qualified experts who ensure you get the services you require. Our services are unique as we aim at customizing them to the customer’s needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Church Administrator

Job Location: Ajao Estate, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Administration
Reports To: Senior Pastor

Job Summary
     

            
    



The Church Administrator is responsible for overseeing the day-to-day administrative and operational functions of the church to ensure efficient service delivery and support the church's mission and vision. The role coordinates church operations, manages administrative staff and volunteers, maintains records, oversees facilities, supports church events, and ensures that administrative processes are effective, organized, and compliant with church policies.

Key Responsibilities
Administrative Management:


	Oversee the daily administrative operations of the church.
	Develop, implement, and maintain administrative policies and procedures.
	Maintain accurate church records, including membership databases, correspondence, and official documentation.
	Prepare reports, meeting minutes, and administrative documents as required.
	Manage the church calendar and coordinate scheduling of services, meetings, and special events.


Office Management:


	Supervise office operations to ensure efficiency and professionalism.
	Manage office supplies, equipment, and vendor relationships.
	Ensure proper filing systems (physical and electronic) are maintained.
	Respond to inquiries from members, visitors, and external stakeholders promptly and professionally.
	Support staff welfare and facilitate performance review processes.
	Coordinate volunteers for church programs and events.
	Ensure volunteers understand their roles and receive necessary support.


Finance & Asset Administration:


	Work closely with the Finance Department to monitor administrative budgets.
	Maintain inventory of church assets and equipment.
	Coordinate procurement processes in accordance with church policies.
	Monitor maintenance schedules for church properties and equipment.


Facility & Logistics Management:


	Ensure church facilities are clean, safe, functional, and well maintained.
	Coordinate repairs, maintenance, and renovation projects.
	Oversee logistics for church services, conferences, weddings, funerals, and other events.
	Ensure venue setup aligns with the requirements of each church program.


Event Coordination:


	Support planning and execution of church events and special programs.
	Coordinate logistics with ministry leaders and service departments.
	Ensure resources and facilities are available for successful program delivery.


Communication & Member Support:


	Serve as the primary administrative contact for members and visitors.
	Coordinate internal communication with ministries and departments.
	Assist in communicating church announcements and administrative updates.
	Maintain confidentiality when handling sensitive church information.


Compliance & Governance:


	Ensure church operations comply with applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
	Support church leadership during audits, inspections, and governance meetings.
	Maintain licenses, permits, and other statutory documentation where applicable.
	Ensure adherence to church policies, safeguarding guidelines, and operational standards.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's degree in any related discipline.
	Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in administration, office management, church administration, or a related role.


Knowledge & Skills:


	Strong administrative and organizational skills.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Strong interpersonal and relationship management skills.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.
	Ability to prioritize multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
	Problem-solving and decision-making skills.
	Strong planning and coordination abilities.
	Working knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Experience with church management software is an added advantage.


Personal Attributes:


	Demonstrates Christian values, integrity, and professionalism.
	Passionate about serving people and supporting ministry work.
	Highly organized and detail-oriented.
	Proactive with a strong sense of responsibility.
	Flexible and willing to work during weekends, church services, and special events when required.
	Ability to work collaboratively with pastors, church leaders, staff, volunteers, and congregation members.
	Maintains discretion when handling confidential information.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Audit Manager",
  "description": "Proforce Industries Limited was incorporated in 2008 as a total defence solution provider specializing in armoured vehicles and personal protection. The company&rsquo;s focus is on the delivery of confidence in protective mobility. We have developed a vibrant market for security and mobile protective products within Nigeria and Africa. PROFORCE has exploited its deep technical know-how to produce outstanding armored products for Government Agencies, Corporate Bodies, Diplomatic Communities and Private individuals within Nigeria and Africa.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Internal Audit Manager

Job Location: No 1 Akaka Road, Ode Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Internal Audit
Industry: Automobile Manufacturing

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and highly analytical Internal Audit Manager to lead our Internal Audit function.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for developing and implementing audit strategies, evaluating internal controls, managing risk, ensuring regulatory compliance, and improving operational efficiency across the organization.
	The ideal candidate should possess strong leadership, financial, and operational auditing experience, preferably within a manufacturing environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement the annual internal audit plan based on organizational risk assessments.
	Lead financial, operational, compliance, and inventory audits across all departments.
	Evaluate the effectiveness of internal controls, governance processes, and risk management systems.
	Review financial records, procurement activities, inventory management, production processes, and operational procedures.
	Identify control weaknesses, operational inefficiencies, and areas of potential fraud or financial loss.
	Prepare detailed audit reports with findings, recommendations, and corrective action plans.
	Monitor the implementation of audit recommendations and verify corrective actions.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, accounting standards, and statutory regulations.
	Provide advisory support to management on internal control improvements and business process optimization.
	Supervise and mentor Internal Audit Officers and other audit personnel.
	Liaise with external auditors and regulatory agencies during statutory audits and inspections.
	Promote a culture of accountability, compliance, and continuous improvement throughout the organization.


Qualifications and Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
	7&ndash;10 years of relevant experience in Internal Audit, External Audit, Risk Management, or Compliance, with at least 3 years in a managerial or supervisory role.
	Master&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration (MBA), or a related field will be an added advantage.
	Professional certification such as ACA, ACCA, CIA, ANAN, CISA, or equivalent is highly desirable.
	Experience in the manufacturing or industrial sector is highly preferred.
	Strong knowledge of auditing standards, internal control frameworks, enterprise risk management, and corporate governance.
	Excellent analytical, investigative, leadership, and report-writing skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, and experience with ERP systems is an added advantage.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and professional ethics.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
	Professional development and leadership opportunities.
	A collaborative and innovative working environment.
	Career growth within a leading automobile manufacturing company.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: hr@proforcedefence.com and copy: hrm@proforcedefence.com using &quot;Application for Internal Audit Manager&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Internal Audit Manager Job at Proforce Industries Limited




        
    

        
        Proforce Industries Limited was incorporated in 2008 as a total defence solution provider specializing in armoured vehicles and personal protection. The company’s focus is on the delivery of confidence in protective mobility. We have developed a vibrant market for security and mobile protective products within Nigeria and Africa. PROFORCE has exploited its deep technical know-how to produce outstanding armored products for Government Agencies, Corporate Bodies, Diplomatic Communities and Private individuals within Nigeria and Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Internal Audit Manager
     

            
    



Job Location: No 1 Akaka Road, Ode Remo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Internal Audit
Industry: Automobile Manufacturing

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and highly analytical Internal Audit Manager to lead our Internal Audit function.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for developing and implementing audit strategies, evaluating internal controls, managing risk, ensuring regulatory compliance, and improving operational efficiency across the organization.
	The ideal candidate should possess strong leadership, financial, and operational auditing experience, preferably within a manufacturing environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement the annual internal audit plan based on organizational risk assessments.
	Lead financial, operational, compliance, and inventory audits across all departments.
	Evaluate the effectiveness of internal controls, governance processes, and risk management systems.
	Review financial records, procurement activities, inventory management, production processes, and operational procedures.
	Identify control weaknesses, operational inefficiencies, and areas of potential fraud or financial loss.
	Prepare detailed audit reports with findings, recommendations, and corrective action plans.
	Monitor the implementation of audit recommendations and verify corrective actions.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, accounting standards, and statutory regulations.
	Provide advisory support to management on internal control improvements and business process optimization.
	Supervise and mentor Internal Audit Officers and other audit personnel.
	Liaise with external auditors and regulatory agencies during statutory audits and inspections.
	Promote a culture of accountability, compliance, and continuous improvement throughout the organization.


Qualifications and Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
	7–10 years of relevant experience in Internal Audit, External Audit, Risk Management, or Compliance, with at least 3 years in a managerial or supervisory role.
	Master's Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration (MBA), or a related field will be an added advantage.
	Professional certification such as ACA, ACCA, CIA, ANAN, CISA, or equivalent is highly desirable.
	Experience in the manufacturing or industrial sector is highly preferred.
	Strong knowledge of auditing standards, internal control frameworks, enterprise risk management, and corporate governance.
	Excellent analytical, investigative, leadership, and report-writing skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, and experience with ERP systems is an added advantage.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and professional ethics.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
	Professional development and leadership opportunities.
	A collaborative and innovative working environment.
	Career growth within a leading automobile manufacturing company.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] and copy: [email protected] using "Application for Internal Audit Manager" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Marketing Officer",
  "description": "Edo Heritage Hotel Benin City, is a four star hotel with large upscale establishments, fully staffed and complete with tons of extras. Edo Heritage Hotel, one of the leading hotels in Benin City offers you services that will last in your memories, as we are fully aware that after a long journey and stressful day at work, you need to wind down in serenity for peace of mind. Edo heritage hotel stands for recreation, relaxation, comfort, luxury and excellence with modern facilities.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Officer

Job Location: Benin City, Edo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Marketing Officer is responsible for increasing the visibility, occupancy, and revenue of Edo Heritage Hotel by developing and executing strategic marketing initiatives.
	The role involves promoting the hotel&#39;s accommodation, restaurant, bush bar, event facilities, and other services through digital marketing, corporate sales, partnerships, and community engagement.


Key Responsibilities
Marketing &amp; Brand Management:


	Develop and implement monthly and annual marketing plans.
	Promote all hotel services, including rooms, restaurant, bush bar, event halls, and special packages.
	Ensure all marketing materials reflect the Edo Heritage Hotel brand.
	Coordinate seasonal promotions, festive campaigns, and special events.


Digital Marketing:


	Manage the hotel&#39;s social media platforms (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, and X).
	Create engaging content including photos, videos, reels, and promotional campaigns.
	Update the hotel website with current offers, events, menus, and room information.
	Monitor online reviews and respond professionally to guest feedback.


Sales &amp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Business Development:


	Identify and secure new corporate clients, travel agencies, government agencies, NGOs, schools, and event planners.
	Build and maintain relationships with existing clients.
	Develop strategies to increase conference bookings, accommodation sales, restaurant patronage, and outdoor catering opportunities.
	Conduct regular sales visits to prospective clients.


Market Research:


	Monitor competitors&#39; pricing, promotions, and services.
	Research customer trends and identify new market opportunities.
	Recommend pricing and promotional strategies based on market intelligence.


Advertising &amp; Promotion:


	Work with designers, photographers, and media agencies to produce marketing materials.
	Organize hotel promotional events and community engagement activities.


Performance Reporting:


	Prepare weekly and monthly marketing reports.
	Track campaign performance using key metrics such as occupancy, bookings, revenue generated, website traffic, social media engagement, and return on marketing investment.
	Recommend improvements based on campaign performance.


Customer Relationship Management:


	Maintain a database of corporate clients and repeat guests.
	Coordinate email marketing campaigns and promotional newsletters.
	Work closely with Front Office, Food &amp; Beverage, and Events teams to ensure guest satisfaction.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Increase room occupancy.
	Increase food and beverage sales.
	Growth in corporate accounts.
	Growth in event and conference bookings.
	Social media engagement and follower growth.
	Website traffic and online inquiries.
	Marketing campaign return on investment (ROI).
	Guest review ratings and online reputation.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Marketing, Mass Communication, Business Administration, or HND
	Minimum of 2 years of marketing experience, preferably in hospitality.
	Professional certifications in Digital Marketing are an added advantage.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong digital marketing and social media management skills.
	Graphic design and content creation experience (Canva, Adobe Creative Suite, or similar) is an advantage.
	Photography and videography skills are desirable.
	Knowledge of SEO, Google Business Profile, and email marketing.
	Strong negotiation and sales skills.
	Excellent presentation and networking abilities.
	Analytical and reporting skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@edoheritagehotel.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.&nbsp;
Or
Submit Application by hand at:
Edo Heritage Hotel,
4. Osadolor Omoregie Street by Okundia Junction,
off 2nd Ugbor Road, GRA,
Benin City, Edo State.",

Marketing Officer Job at Edo Heritage Hotel




        
    

        
        Edo Heritage Hotel Benin City, is a four star hotel with large upscale establishments, fully staffed and complete with tons of extras. Edo Heritage Hotel, one of the leading hotels in Benin City offers you services that will last in your memories, as we are fully aware that after a long journey and stressful day at work, you need to wind down in serenity for peace of mind. Edo heritage hotel stands for recreation, relaxation, comfort, luxury and excellence with modern facilities.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Officer

Job Location: Benin City, Edo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Marketing Officer is responsible for increasing the visibility, occupancy, and revenue of Edo Heritage Hotel by developing and executing strategic marketing initiatives.
	The role involves promoting the hotel's accommodation, restaurant, bush bar, event facilities, and other services through digital marketing, corporate sales, partnerships, and community engagement.


Key Responsibilities
Marketing & Brand Management:


	Develop and implement monthly and annual marketing plans.
	Promote all hotel services, including rooms, restaurant, bush bar, event halls, and special packages.
	Ensure all marketing materials reflect the Edo Heritage Hotel brand.
	Coordinate seasonal promotions, festive campaigns, and special events.


Digital Marketing:


	Manage the hotel's social media platforms (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, and X).
	Create engaging content including photos, videos, reels, and promotional campaigns.
	Update the hotel website with current offers, events, menus, and room information.
	Monitor online reviews and respond professionally to guest feedback.


Sales &  Business Development:


	Identify and secure new corporate clients, travel agencies, government agencies, NGOs, schools, and event planners.
	Build and maintain relationships with existing clients.
	Develop strategies to increase conference bookings, accommodation sales, restaurant patronage, and outdoor catering opportunities.
	Conduct regular sales visits to prospective clients.


Market Research:


	Monitor competitors' pricing, promotions, and services.
	Research customer trends and identify new market opportunities.
	Recommend pricing and promotional strategies based on market intelligence.


Advertising & Promotion:


	Work with designers, photographers, and media agencies to produce marketing materials.
	Organize hotel promotional events and community engagement activities.


Performance Reporting:


	Prepare weekly and monthly marketing reports.
	Track campaign performance using key metrics such as occupancy, bookings, revenue generated, website traffic, social media engagement, and return on marketing investment.
	Recommend improvements based on campaign performance.


Customer Relationship Management:


	Maintain a database of corporate clients and repeat guests.
	Coordinate email marketing campaigns and promotional newsletters.
	Work closely with Front Office, Food & Beverage, and Events teams to ensure guest satisfaction.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Increase room occupancy.
	Increase food and beverage sales.
	Growth in corporate accounts.
	Growth in event and conference bookings.
	Social media engagement and follower growth.
	Website traffic and online inquiries.
	Marketing campaign return on investment (ROI).
	Guest review ratings and online reputation.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Mass Communication, Business Administration, or HND
	Minimum of 2 years of marketing experience, preferably in hospitality.
	Professional certifications in Digital Marketing are an added advantage.


Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong digital marketing and social media management skills.
	Graphic design and content creation experience (Canva, Adobe Creative Suite, or similar) is an advantage.
	Photography and videography skills are desirable.
	Knowledge of SEO, Google Business Profile, and email marketing.
	Strong negotiation and sales skills.
	Excellent presentation and networking abilities.
	Analytical and reporting skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail. 
Or
Submit Application by hand at:
Edo Heritage Hotel,
4. Osadolor Omoregie Street by Okundia Junction,
off 2nd Ugbor Road, GRA,
Benin City, Edo State.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Compliance Officer",
  "description": "African Industries Group (AIG) is a diversified global conglomerate with a legacy of 52 years of business excellence and sustainable growth. It is headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria. It has been contributing to Nigeria&rsquo;s socio-economic growth and industrial development and is equally committed towards the nurturing and development of the local community.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Compliance officer&nbsp;

Job Location: Agbara, Ogun&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Compliance Officer will be responsible for ensuring that the Agbara plantoperates in full compliance with all applicable statutory, regulatory, and internal company requirements.
	The role will support regulatory governance by ensuring timely statutory filings, maintaining compliance records, liaising with government agencies, conducting compliance monitoring, and advising management on regulatory obligations to minimize business and legal risks.


Key Responsibilities


	Ensure the organization complies with all applicable federal, state, and local laws, regulations, and industry standards.
	Monitor changes in regulatory requirements and communicate their impact to relevant stakeholders.
	Coordinate and facilitate timely statutory filings, licence renewals, permits, and regulatory submissions.
	Liaise with government agencies and regulatory bodies, including but not limited to:
	NESREA
	SON
	NAFDAC (where applicable)
	Fire Service and other relevant regulatory agencies.
	Maintain an up-to-date compliance register, statutory records, licences, permits, and certificates.
	Conduct periodic compliance audits across departments to identify potential compliance gaps and recommend corrective actions.
	Develop and monitor compliance checklists to ensure adherence to statutory and internal policy requirements.
	Support internal and external audits by providing required documentation and coordinating audit responses.
	Prepare periodic compliance reports and present findings to management.
	Investigate compliance issues and recommend preventive and corrective actions.
	&nbsp;Work closely with HR, Finance, Operations, Legal, Procurement, HSE, and other departments to ensure regulatory compliance across business activities.
	Coordinate compliance awareness sessions and educate employees on regulatory requirements and company policies.
	Maintain proper documentation for inspections, regulatory visits, and compliance-related correspondence.
	Ensure prompt closure of regulatory observations, audit findings, and compliance action plans.
	Perform any other compliance-related duties assigned by management.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Law, Business Administration, Accounting, Public Administration, Political Science, Industrial Relations, or a related discipline.
	Professional certification in Compliance, Risk Management, Internal Audit, Corporate Governance, or related fields will be an added advantage.
	Membership of relevant professional bodies is desirable.


Experience:


	5&ndash;10years of relevant experience in Compliance, Internal Audit, Risk Management, Corporate Governance, Legal Compliance, or Regulatory Affairs.
	Experience within a manufacturing or Chemical Industry is highly preferred.
	Experience dealing directly with government and regulatory agencies will be an added advantage.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of Nigerian statutory and regulatory requirements.
	Excellent understanding of corporate governance and compliance frameworks.
	Knowledge of compliance monitoring and risk assessment methodologies.


Salary
Negotiable.

Method of Applictaion&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: Recruitment.Agbara@Africanindustries.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Compliance Officer Job at African Industries Group




        
    

        
        African Industries Group (AIG) is a diversified global conglomerate with a legacy of 52 years of business excellence and sustainable growth. It is headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria. It has been contributing to Nigeria’s socio-economic growth and industrial development and is equally committed towards the nurturing and development of the local community.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Compliance officer 

Job Location: Agbara, Ogun 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Compliance Officer will be responsible for ensuring that the Agbara plantoperates in full compliance with all applicable statutory, regulatory, and internal company requirements.
	The role will support regulatory governance by ensuring timely statutory filings, maintaining compliance records, liaising with government agencies, conducting compliance monitoring, and advising management on regulatory obligations to minimize business and legal risks.


Key Responsibilities


	Ensure the organization complies with all applicable federal, state, and local laws, regulations, and industry standards.
	Monitor changes in regulatory requirements and communicate their impact to relevant stakeholders.
	Coordinate and facilitate timely statutory filings, licence renewals, permits, and regulatory submissions.
	Liaise with government agencies and regulatory bodies, including but not limited to:
	NESREA
	SON
	NAFDAC (where applicable)
	Fire Service and other relevant regulatory agencies.
	Maintain an up-to-date compliance register, statutory records, licences, permits, and certificates.
	Conduct periodic compliance audits across departments to identify potential compliance gaps and recommend corrective actions.
	Develop and monitor compliance checklists to ensure adherence to statutory and internal policy requirements.
	Support internal and external audits by providing required documentation and coordinating audit responses.
	Prepare periodic compliance reports and present findings to management.
	Investigate compliance issues and recommend preventive and corrective actions.
	 Work closely with HR, Finance, Operations, Legal, Procurement, HSE, and other departments to ensure regulatory compliance across business activities.
	Coordinate compliance awareness sessions and educate employees on regulatory requirements and company policies.
	Maintain proper documentation for inspections, regulatory visits, and compliance-related correspondence.
	Ensure prompt closure of regulatory observations, audit findings, and compliance action plans.
	Perform any other compliance-related duties assigned by management.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Law, Business Administration, Accounting, Public Administration, Political Science, Industrial Relations, or a related discipline.
	Professional certification in Compliance, Risk Management, Internal Audit, Corporate Governance, or related fields will be an added advantage.
	Membership of relevant professional bodies is desirable.


Experience:


	5–10years of relevant experience in Compliance, Internal Audit, Risk Management, Corporate Governance, Legal Compliance, or Regulatory Affairs.
	Experience within a manufacturing or Chemical Industry is highly preferred.
	Experience dealing directly with government and regulatory agencies will be an added advantage.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of Nigerian statutory and regulatory requirements.
	Excellent understanding of corporate governance and compliance frameworks.
	Knowledge of compliance monitoring and risk assessment methodologies.


Salary
Negotiable.

Method of Applictaion 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Personal Assistant",
  "description": "Bridgemead Consulting is a holistic and innovative business consultancy that believes in connecting businesses with the right individuals. We take pride in not just connecting clients to skilled talent but also ensuring we provide personalised solutions to all Human Resource challenges.

At Bridgemead we offer professional and unique Human Resources solutions to facilitate specific business requirements, enabling you focus solely on your business. We are a fully outsourced platform for Small Medium Enterprises (SME&rsquo;s) and our services span the full spectrum of all HR relayed business priorities. All our experts are CIPM, CIPD, SPHRI qualified with vast experience of real &ndash; life business processes. Our team of dedicated professionals guarantee efficient, attainable and effective results that will positively transform your business processes internally and externally irrespective of size.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Personal Assistant
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time (Hybrid: Minimum of 3 in-office/site days per week)
Reports To: Executive Assistant
Gender: Female Preferred.

About the Role


	We are seeking a highly organized, proactive, and discreet Personal Assistant to provide dedicated support in managing the executive&#39;s personal affairs, household operations, and lifestyle commitments.
	The successful candidate will ensure the executive&#39;s personal life runs efficiently by coordinating schedules, managing errands, overseeing household activities, and handling confidential matters with professionalism.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the executive&#39;s personal calendar, appointments, reminders, social engagements, and reservations.
	Coordinate personal errands, purchases, and administrative tasks while sourcing requested products and services.
	Liaise with domestic staff, contractors, vendors, and service providers, ensuring household operations, supplies, deliveries, and maintenance activities run smoothly.
	Anticipate the executive&#39;s needs, resolve issues proactively, and handle ad hoc requests with efficiency and discretion.
	Work closely with the Executive Assistant to ensure seamless coordination between the executive&#39;s personal and professional schedules.
	Maintain the highest level of confidentiality, integrity, and professionalism in handling personal and family matters.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Timely and accurate coordination of the executive&#39;s personal schedule and activities.
	Efficient completion of assigned tasks with minimal supervision.
	Smooth management of household operations and personal logistics.
	High standards of confidentiality, responsiveness, and stakeholder satisfaction.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Management, or a related field is preferred.
	2&ndash;4 years of experience as a Personal Assistant, Executive Assistant, or Administrative Assistant.
	Previous experience supporting senior executives is an advantage.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Excellent organizational, time management, and multitasking skills.
	Strong communication, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.
	High attention to detail with the ability to work independently and adapt to changing priorities.
	Exceptional discretion, integrity, and professionalism.


Working Arrangement:


	This is a hybrid role requiring a minimum of three in-person working days each week across the executive&#39;s office and residence, depending on operational needs. Occasional availability outside standard working hours may be required to support urgent personal matters.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@bridgemeadadvisory.com using &quot;Personal Assistant&quot; &nbsp;as the subject of the email.",

  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"

Personal Assistant Job at Bridgemead Consulting




        
    

        
        Bridgemead Consulting is a holistic and innovative business consultancy that believes in connecting businesses with the right individuals. We take pride in not just connecting clients to skilled talent but also ensuring we provide personalised solutions to all Human Resource challenges.
     

            
    



At Bridgemead we offer professional and unique Human Resources solutions to facilitate specific business requirements, enabling you focus solely on your business. We are a fully outsourced platform for Small Medium Enterprises (SME’s) and our services span the full spectrum of all HR relayed business priorities. All our experts are CIPM, CIPD, SPHRI qualified with vast experience of real – life business processes. Our team of dedicated professionals guarantee efficient, attainable and effective results that will positively transform your business processes internally and externally irrespective of size.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Personal Assistant
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time (Hybrid: Minimum of 3 in-office/site days per week)
Reports To: Executive Assistant
Gender: Female Preferred.

About the Role


	We are seeking a highly organized, proactive, and discreet Personal Assistant to provide dedicated support in managing the executive's personal affairs, household operations, and lifestyle commitments.
	The successful candidate will ensure the executive's personal life runs efficiently by coordinating schedules, managing errands, overseeing household activities, and handling confidential matters with professionalism.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the executive's personal calendar, appointments, reminders, social engagements, and reservations.
	Coordinate personal errands, purchases, and administrative tasks while sourcing requested products and services.
	Liaise with domestic staff, contractors, vendors, and service providers, ensuring household operations, supplies, deliveries, and maintenance activities run smoothly.
	Anticipate the executive's needs, resolve issues proactively, and handle ad hoc requests with efficiency and discretion.
	Work closely with the Executive Assistant to ensure seamless coordination between the executive's personal and professional schedules.
	Maintain the highest level of confidentiality, integrity, and professionalism in handling personal and family matters.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Timely and accurate coordination of the executive's personal schedule and activities.
	Efficient completion of assigned tasks with minimal supervision.
	Smooth management of household operations and personal logistics.
	High standards of confidentiality, responsiveness, and stakeholder satisfaction.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Management, or a related field is preferred.
	2–4 years of experience as a Personal Assistant, Executive Assistant, or Administrative Assistant.
	Previous experience supporting senior executives is an advantage.


Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent organizational, time management, and multitasking skills.
	Strong communication, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.
	High attention to detail with the ability to work independently and adapt to changing priorities.
	Exceptional discretion, integrity, and professionalism.


Working Arrangement:


	This is a hybrid role requiring a minimum of three in-person working days each week across the executive's office and residence, depending on operational needs. Occasional availability outside standard working hours may be required to support urgent personal matters.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Personal Assistant"  as the subject of the email.
